description: A real candid outdoor photograph (not a designed graphic) taken from the base of the steps of the Frances Perkins Building, headquarters of the U.S. Department of Labor in Washington, D.C. The shot looks up at the concrete brutalist facade under a blue sky with scattered clouds. Hanging on the building is a very large vertical banner bearing a stern portrait photo of President Trump and the partially visible text 'AMERICA 250' and 'AMERICAN WORKERS FIRST'. A large American flag is draped vertically on the right side of the facade, and another U.S. flag flies from a pole. In the foreground, out of focus, a blonde woman in a white top (back to camera) holds up a smartphone to photograph the banner, and a second person at right also photographs it. The building name 'DEPARTMENT OF LABOR / FRANCES PERKINS BUILDING' is faintly legible on the facade. The tweet caption ('did I catch your eye?') frames it as a self-promotional image of the agency's Trump-branding of its own headquarters. Although the building display is propaganda, the image itself is a plain candid photograph with minimal added design, so is_designed=false.
notable_text: AMERICAN WORKERS FIRST; AMERICA 250 (on the building banner)
